content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                    Date : October 17, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight E. Dickerson

Subject: Chris Burns' letter to the FPPC dated September 25, 1978

     The question raised in Mr. Chris Burns' letter was whether
the Emeryville City Attorney was disqualified from participating
in Planning Commission decisions affecting the Watergate Tenants
Association (Association). Mr. Burns, President of the Association, was concerned that the City Attorney may have a conflict
of interest because of the Attorney's pending libel suit against
the Association whereby he is seeking damages of $6 million.
It is our understanding from Mr. Burns that the City Attorney has
not received nor has been promised income aggregating $250 or
more from the Association.

     I responded to Mr. Burns' inquiry by telephone. I informed
him that the filing of a law suit by the City Attorney seeking
damages from the Association did not make the Association a
source of income to the City Attorney. Based upon these considerations, I told Mr. Burns that §§87100 and 87103 of the Act
does not prohibit the City Attorney from participating in
Planning Commission discussions which may affect the Association.

DED:nrw

WATERGATE TENANTS ASSOCIATION
6-A ADMIRAL DRIVE, #481
Emeryville, California 94608

September 25, 1978

Mr. Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Prim:

In addition to previous correspondence and discussions we have had with you
about certain political activities about the Watergate Company's application
for a tentative subdivision map in Emeryville, we have additional inquiries.
Please be aware that this is only an inquiry about a public official performing
official duties - it has nothing to do with his private life or him personally.

The immediate question is, is the City Attorney in a Conflict of Interest
position with respect to the Emeryville City Council meeting to be held this
Thursday, September 28th to consider final approval of the Watergate tentative
subdivision map? The question arises because he presently has a lawsuit pending
in Superior Court, Alameda County, against 650 individuals who have a direct in-
terest in the matter being considered.

The facts are:

1. On August 25th Mr. James Stanford White, City Attorney in Emeryville,
entered a lawsuit in Superior Court, Alameda County, against myself,
against the Watergate Tenants Association and against Does 1 through
650. The suit seeks punitive damages of $6.5 million. A copy is
attached.

2. Watergate Tenants Association is a voluntary organization representing
all people who rent apartments at Watergate. It has existed for about
five years. There are no dues, but there are, from time to time,
various social and business meetings.

3. The 650 Does presumably are the approximately 650 people who signed
petitions relative to the matter being considered at recent Emeryville
City meetings. There were two forms of petitions - a copy of each is
attached. The petitions had been presented to the City Clerk on
August 22, 1978, so the identities could have been ascertained
before August 25th.

Committees: Security □ Maintenance □ Education and Bylaws □ Recreational and Social Activities

Mr. Ted Prim
September 25, 1978
Page Two


Further, as to the letters to the State Bar which are the subject of the law
suit, they were merely inquiries about a public official's performance of his
official City duties. There were not intended to raise any questions about
the man's character or personal life, nor were they intended to convey the
idea that the man is dishonest or self-serving. My attorney's opinion is
that the suit was an attempt at intimidation.

One more point. Mr. White was just appointed as permanent City Attorney at
the City Council Meeting last Tuesday, September 22nd. We think that Mr.
White probably has a conflict of interest in performing such City respon-
sibilities at the same time that the above mentioned suit against a
significant number of Emeryville residents is pending in Superior Court.

We would like your opinion.

We would like your consideration of this question prior to the meeting
this Thursday, September 28th.

Sincerely yours,

[illegible]

Chris Burns
President
Watergate Tenants Association

CB:rms
Encls.

sections.question: Whether the Emeryville City Attorney was disqualified from participating in Planning Commission decisions affecting Watergate Tenants Association, given his pending libel suit against the Association seeking $6 million in damages.
sections.conclusion: The potential receipt of damages from the Association as a source of income to the City Attorney, under the circumstances of these conflicts and sections 3650 and 3671.3 of the Political Reform Act, does not prohibit the City Attorney from participating in Planning Commission decisions affecting the Association.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Emeryville City Attorney have a disqualifying conflict of interest that prevents him from participating in Planning Commission decisions affecting the Watergate Tenants Association when he has a pending $6 million libel suit against that organization?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the City Attorney's pending libel suit against the Watergate Tenants Association does not disqualify him from participating in Planning Commission decisions, as the potential damages would not constitute sufficient income under the Political Reform Act to create a legal conflict of interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is partially illegible with scanning artifacts and formatting issues. The memorandum from FPPC and the incoming request letter from Chris Burns are both present. The conclusion appears incomplete in the source document. Confidence reduced due to text quality and partial illegibility of key sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Emeryville City Attorney was disqualified from participating in Planning Commission decisions affecting Watergate Tenants Association, given his pending libel suit against the Association seeking $6 million in damages.

CONCLUSION: The potential receipt of damages from the Association as a source of income to the City Attorney, under the circumstances of these conflicts and sections 3650 and 3671.3 of the Political Reform Act, does not prohibit the City Attorney from participating in Planning Commission decisions affecting the Association.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding whether the Emeryville City Attorney has a conflict of interest precluding his participation in Planning Commission decisions affecting the Watergate Tenants Association, with which he has a pending $6 million libel suit. The FPPC concluded no disqualification exists under the Political Reform Act.
